input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's a complete `AppShell.xaml` setup:

**AppShell.xaml**
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyApp.Views"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, no tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**
```csharp
namespace MyApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register the detail page route (not in the flyout/visual hierarchy)
        Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(Views.ProductDetailsPage), typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Navigating to the detail page** (e.g., from `ActiveProductsPage`):

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

**Key points:**

- `FlyoutItem` creates a flyout menu entry; nesting multiple `<Tab>` elements inside one gives you the tab bar.
- `ShellContent` with `ContentTemplate` uses lazy loading — pages aren't created until visited.
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` is needed for pages that aren't part of the visual hierarchy (like detail/push pages).
- Pass parameters via query strings and receive them with `[QueryProperty]` or `IQueryAttributable`.
data.reasoningText:
The user wants help setting up a .NET MAUI AppShell.xaml with a specific navigation structure. They need:

1. A flyout menu with:
   - "Dashboard" (single page)
   - "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
   - "Settings" page
2. Route registration for a ProductDetailsPage

I'll provide the XAML and the code-behind with route registration.
